business.headline: Need fairness: Supreme Court quashes CCI's Rs 202 crore penalty on AmazonThe Supreme Court set aside the CCI's Rs 202 crore penalty on Amazon and quashed orders suspending its Future Coupons deal. The ruling ends a five-year legal battle and underscores that regulators must act with legality, fairness and reasoned decision-making.

Hiranandani says India's affordable housing crisis is getting worse. Here's proofFor many aspiring homeowners, the biggest challenge today is no longer deciding where to buy a house, it is finding one that fits the budget. As affordable housing supply drops, experts warn that the problem is becoming harder to ignore.

US delegation to visit India from June 1–4 to finalise interim trade pactThe discussions are expected to focus on important areas such as market access, non-tariff measures, customs and trade facilitation, investment promotion and economic security alignment.

Sensex slips 142 points, Nifty ends flat as auto and metal stocks support marketThe S&P BSE Sensex fell 141.90 points, or 0.19%, to close at 75,867.80. Meanwhile, the NSE Nifty50 slipped just 6.55 points, or 0.03%, to settle at 23,907.15.

Air India to cut domestic flights by 22% as Iran war pushes fuel costs higherThe cuts will affect Air India's domestic operations for June and July, while decisions for August are yet to be finalised.

HDFC Bank responds to Rs 45 crore payment allegations, denies 'wrongdoing'HDFC Bank shares fell sharply during Wednesday's trading session. The stock dropped nearly 2.6%, trading at Rs 758.75 on the NSE around 1:29 pm, down Rs 20.15.

HDFC Bank shares drop 2% on report of probe into Rs 45-crore interest paymentsThe stock dropped nearly 2.6% during the trading session after the report surfaced. As per NSE data cited in the report, HDFC Bank shares were trading at Rs 758.75, down Rs 20.15 or 2.59%, around 1:29 pm.

8th Pay Commission: Why a union wants 5 different fitment factors instead of oneThe Indian Railway Technical Supervisors' Association (IRTSA) has urged the 8th Pay Commission to replace a single fitment factor with five graded multipliers. The demand shifts the discussion from overall salary hikes to how different levels should be paid.

Air India trims domestic flights by 15–20% during July–August as fuel costs soarIn a statement, an Air India spokesperson said the airline has temporarily adjusted some domestic services after earlier changes to international routes.

How AI helped Taiwan overtake India to become the world's 5th largest stock marketTaiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company is the world's largest contract chipmaker and plays a major role in producing advanced semiconductors used in AI systems.

Not a finding of fraud, dishonesty: Byju Raveendran on jail sentence in SingaporeResponding to the developments, Raveendran said the matter has been misunderstood and is part of ongoing settlement talks with lenders and stakeholders.

From Messi to mounting lawsuits: Inside the collapse of Byju's empireA Singapore court sentenced Byju Raveendran to six months in jail for contempt over asset-related orders. The ruling sharpens focus on Byju's steep fall from a $22 billion edtech darling to a company facing debt, lawsuits and insolvency proceedings.

Air India, BigBasket, Tata Electronics: Inside Tata's key boardroom discussionsTata Sons' latest board meeting stayed calm as operating chiefs answered questions on Air India, BigBasket and other businesses. The discussions showed tighter scrutiny of profits, capital allocation and the group's long-term strategic bets.

Sensex, Nifty open flat as Middle East tensions keep investors cautiousThe S&P BSE Sensex slipped 22.10 points, or 0.03%, to 75,987.60 in early trade, while the NSE Nifty50 declined 6.95 points, or 0.03%, to 23,906.75.

Byju Raveendran sentenced to six months in jail by Singapore courtByju Raveendran, founder of embattled edtech company Byju's, has been sentenced to six months in jail by a Singapore court for contempt of court.

8th Pay Commission: Odisha consultations scheduled for July 6-7The 8th Pay Commission will visit Bhubaneswar on July 6 and 7 for consultations with organisations, unions and employee associations. The outreach comes as the panel widens regional talks and gathers demands on pay, allowances and pensions.

Indian economy is quite robust: Arvind PanagariyaIn an exclusive interview with India Today, Arvind Panagariya, Chairman of the sixteenth Finance Commission, asserts that the domestic economy remains fundamentally robust, citing a steady gross foreign direct investment of ninety-four billion dollars for the recent fiscal year and consistent growth averaging over seven percent in the past three years.

When centralised manufacturing, record irregularities conveniently escaped detectionIn the eighth part of this series on labour laws and Inspector Raj, this article examines how seemingly separate establishments functioned as one operational unit, while material irregularities in records and manufacturing activity went unnoticed.

Hidden cost of AI adoption: Why enterprises are worried about token economicsEnterprises are reassessing AI roll-outs as token and compute costs climb with wider internal use. The shift is pushing companies to focus on cost control, ROI and sustainable scale.

When it rains, it pours: Why Nithin Kamath thinks 2026 could be tough for householdsIn a post on X, Kamath flagged concerns around a possible super El Nino, weak rainfall and rising energy costs linked to global tensions. According to him, if food and fuel prices rise together, inflation could become a much bigger challenge for Indian households.

entertainment.headline: Matthew Perry's assistant gets 41 months in prison for role in actor's death Kenneth Iwamasa had pleaded guilty to one count of conspiracy to distribute ketamine resulting in death. As part of a plea agreement, he admitted to repeatedly injecting Perry with ketamine without medical training, including the lethal dose.

West Bengal CM Suvendu Adhikari pays final respects to filmmaker Anik Dutta West Bengal CM Suvendu Adhikari and members of the Bengali film industry gathered at SSKM Hospital to pay their respects filmmaker Anik Dutta's death, drawing attention across Kolkata.

Marathi actor Mahesh Pawar dies after SUV falls into Raigad gorge Marathi actor Mahesh Pawar died after an SUV plunged into a deep gorge in Maharashtra's Raigad district, leaving seven others dead in the tragic accident.

Karuppu was written as Vijay's final film before Suriya took over: RJ Balaji RJ Balaji said Karuppu was originally developed for Vijay before he entered politics. The film later moved to Suriya, whose input shaped the final script.

Enola Holmes 3 teaser hints at wedding chaos and a kidnapping mystery Actor Millie Bobby Brown returns in the first teaser for Enola Holmes 3 as the young detective heads to Malta for her most dangerous mystery yet.

Kunal Kohli to Ranveer Singh's critics: Don't judge an actor by success or failure Kunal Kohli said Ranveer Singh should not be judged by the success or failure of his films. The filmmaker, while addressing the actor's critics, said flops are inevitable in cinema.

Nick Jonas' latest Bollywood obsession? Vicky Kaushal's Tauba Tauba Nick Jonas said Tauba Tauba is the Bollywood song that gets him dancing at weddings. The remark added to his long-running public affection for Hindi film music and quickly drew attention online.

Imtiaz Ali says Main Vaapas Aaunga is not a political film, it's personal Filmmaker Imtiaz Ali opens up about his upcoming film Main Vaapas Aaunga, saying it is a deeply personal love story set against the backdrop of Partition, rather than a conventional historical drama. Cast members Vedang Raina and Sharvari also share their emotional experiences and sense of responsibility while preparing for their roles.

Sorry but Off-Campus cut Garrett Graham's most iconic book moments. Here are 5 Fans of Off-Campus say the Prime Video adaptation leaves out several defining Garrett Graham and Hannah Wells scenes from The Deal. Here's what the show missed that made the book iconic.

Mika Singh backs Ranveer Singh, says he will speak to film body on Don 3 row Singer Mika Singh backed actor Ranveer Singh in the Don 3 controversy and said he will personally meet and talk to the Federation of Western India Cine Employees. His intervention comes as the casting fallout has escalated into a wider industry dispute.

Ranveer Singh attempted private settlement before Don 3 fallout: Report Actor Ranveer Singh reportedly offered Rs 10 crore upfront and a Rs 25 crore future discount to settle the Don 3 dispute with Excel Entertainment before the fallout escalated publicly.

I asked God for death: Honey Singh opens up on bipolar disorder struggle Rapper-singer Honey Singh recalled asking God for death in 2019 during his battle with bipolar disorder. He said that night, along with treatment and reflection, changed his life and gave him a reason to live.

Priya Patidar on Welcome to the Jungle: Surreal to sing track for Raveena Tandon Priya Patidar said recording the Welcome to the Jungle title track and singing for Raveena Tandon felt surreal. She said the release marked her long-awaited playback breakthrough and carried emotional weight for her family.

Vijay Deverakonda-Rashmika Mandanna airport appearance sparks buzz over his pendant Actors Vijay Deverakonda and Rashmika Mandanna were seen together at the airport in a video that quickly went viral. Fans quickly focused on Vijay's 'R' pendant, linking it to Rashmika ahead of their on-screen reunion in Ranabaali.

Jackie Shroff reacts to son Tiger's box office failures, says not in our hands Jackie Shroff has spoken about son Tiger Shroff's recent box office struggles, saying success and failure are not in an actor's hands. He added that Tiger remains focused on working sincerely and leaving the rest to the audience.

Marriage never changes boundaries: Sonakshi Sinha on filming intimate scenes In an exclusive conversation, actors Sonakshi Sinha and Jyotika opened up about intimacy on screen, personal boundaries, marriage, and working with Ashwiny Iyer Tiwari in System.

Bengali filmmaker Anik Dutta dies at 62 after falling from terrace in Kolkata Filmmaker Anik Dutta died after allegedly falling from a terrace in Kolkata's Hindustan Park area on Wednesday. Police are probing the circumstances, with a suicide note reportedly recovered and his post-mortem pending.

Bhumi Pednekar reacts to alleged assault of 9-year-old girl in UP: Broken society Bhumi Pednekar reacted with shock after a nine-year-old girl was allegedly sexually assaulted by two minor boys in Uttar Pradesh's Ballia district. Calling it a sign of a "broken society", the actor questioned the environment children are growing up in and urged people to take the issue seriously.

Actors body chief Poonam Dhillon says Ranveer 'could have come to us' amid Don 3 row Poonam Dhillon expressed disappointment that Cine and TV Artistes' Association (CINTAA) was not approached in the Ranveer Singh-Don 3 dispute. The association says it cannot take a stand until it knows the full facts.

general news.headline: They owe it to us: Trump hinges Iran deal on Gulf states joining Abraham AccordsDonald Trump ties future Iran negotiations to the expansion of the Abraham Accords. He wants Saudi Arabia, Qatar, Pakistan and Turkey to join the Israel normalisation framework.

Chandrayaan-2 discovers possible ice buried on Moon's South PoleIndia's Chandrayaan-2 orbiter may have uncovered hidden ice beneath some of the Moon's coldest craters, a discovery that could reshape future lunar missions and strengthen hopes of long-term human presence on the Moon.

Political move amid resignation buzz? Siddaramaiah accepts caste survey reportApproving the report could trigger resistance from influential dominant communities, while delaying or rejecting it could draw criticism from backward class groups and AHINDA supporters. Either way, the next government could find itself navigating a politically sensitive situation.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi stands frozen on pitch after missing fastest IPL centuryIPL 2026 Eliminator, SRH vs RR: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi missed the record for the fastest century in the tournament when he was dismissed for 97 off 29 balls. The 15-year-old stood frozen in the middle, unable to believe his luck after he threw it away in the eighth over of the match in Mullanpur on Wednesday.

30 lakh beneficiaries of Mamata's Lakshmir Bhandar unfit for new Bengal schemeWest Bengal Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari said 30 lakh ineligible people were receiving cash benefits under Mamata Banerjee's Lakshmir Bhandar. He said these bogus beneficiaries, including non-Indians, had been weeded out, and won't be receiving benefits under the new Annapurna Bhandar scheme.

After Iran showdown, Trump threatens to blow up Oman for this reasonDonald Trump warns that no country will control the Strait of Hormuz. His statement stuns many because Oman has long been one of Washington's closest and oldest Gulf partners.

Cow sacrifice not essential to Bakrid: Madras High Court orders statewide banThe Madras High Court directed Tamil Nadu to ensure that no cow or calf is slaughtered on the eve of Bakrid or on any other day in the state. The bench said only legally approved slaughter houses can operate and ordered senior officials to enforce the ruling.

Why Siddaramaiah is no pushover even for mighty Congress brassFor at least three years, the Congress high command carefully balanced Siddaramaiah's role as the Karnataka Chief Minister and his deputy DK Shivakumar's aspirations. While DKS might have his CM dream fulfilled at last, the mighty Congress brass walks a risky path in asking Siddu to step aside. Here's why.

India has more cities than it knows of. Why are they hiding in plain sight?India might have far more cities and towns than the government's numbers acknowledge. An EAC-PM paper argues that millions live in human settlements that function like towns and cities but officially remain classified as rural. This adversely impacts the lives of millions of residents.

Matthew Perry's assistant gets 41 months in prison for role in actor's deathKenneth Iwamasa had pleaded guilty to one count of conspiracy to distribute ketamine resulting in death. As part of a plea agreement, he admitted to repeatedly injecting Perry with ketamine without medical training, including the lethal dose.

Poor in India paying more tax due to GST, says LSE expert, faces flackAn anthropologist at the London School of Economics, Mukulika Banerjee, argued that poorer Indians pay a larger share of their income in GST than the rich. Experts, however, countered that most daily-use essentials consumed by poorer households are either GST-free or taxed at just 5%.

Not at all: Trump rules out sanctions relief for Iran uranium handoverUS President Donald Trump ruled out the possibility that the current framework under discussion could involve sanctions relief in exchange for Iran's highly enriched uranium.

How DK Shivakumar outplayed SiddaramaiahSiddaramaiah believed numbers and a strong support base would shield him from any leadership challenge. But DK Shivakumar hit the reset button in Karnataka with the blessings of those who really call the shots -- the Congress high command.

AI giving no gains, tech layoffs happening for no real reason: Sridhar VembuWeeks before top AI leaders started softening their warnings around job losses, Zoho founder Sridhar Vembu was already questioning whether AI was truly delivering real productivity gains. He also suggested companies may be using AI to cut labour cost and impress investors.

Western disturbance to roll in, monsoon to advance on ThursdayA fresh Western Disturbance is set to bring thundersqualls, hail and dust storms across parts of North and central India. The change comes as the southwest monsoon advances further and temperatures in the northwest are forecast to ease.

Tamil Nadu CM Vijay meets PM Modi, raises Vande Mataram row during talksTamil Nadu Chief Minister Vijay's first Delhi visit after taking office turned politically significant as he met PM Modi after 12 years, triggering fresh speculation over equations, alliances and the fast-changing power dynamics in the state.

I asked God for death: Honey Singh opens up on bipolar disorder struggleRapper-singer Honey Singh recalled asking God for death in 2019 during his battle with bipolar disorder. He said that night, along with treatment and reflection, changed his life and gave him a reason to live.

Fame saves 'Trump' buffalo from Eid sacrifice, sent to Bangladesh zooA rare albino buffalo in Bangladesh became an internet sensation because of its blond "Trump-like" hair. His viral fame may have saved its life. Hours before Eid sacrifice, the government stepped in and moved the animal to Dhaka zoo.

El Nino confirmed, alert sounded by Australia: Should India be worried?The Pacific Ocean is changing and the shift that could reshape India's weather is closer than we realise. Here's when a potentially historic El Nino could hit.

French Open, Day 4 LIVE: Djokovic advances to third round as Rybakina crashes outFrench Open 2026, Day 4 Live updates: Novak Djokovic sealed his spot in the third round of the French Open with 6-3, 6-2, 6-7, 6-3 win over Valentin Royer. Iga Swiatek cruised past Sara Bejlek 6-2, 6-3 to continue her title charge, while reigning Australian Open champion Elena Rybakina suffered a shock second-round exit after losing to Yuliia Starodubtseva.

Can sleeping like an astronaut improve your sleep?The world is in it's sleep deprived era and looking for the next best thing to hack their way to having a good night's sleep. The 'zero gravity sleep' position might just help.

Vijay didn't suspend cops for laughing during Coimbatore rape-murder presserThe media relations officer for the Tamil Nadu Police, Balaji, confirmed to India Today Fact Check that any suspension claims are completely baseless.

Get the f*** out of US: Indian couple face racist abuse, incident caught on cameraA video showing an American man telling an Indian couple to "go back to India" during a racist confrontation has received widespread criticism online, with many calling the remarks discriminatory and unprovoked.

Live: Trump says US can make good deal with Iran after claiming regime changeUS-Israel-Iran Talks Live Updates: US President Donald Trump is set to convene a Cabinet meeting as pressure grows to secure a breakthrough in the Iran conflict, though diplomats familiar with the talks say there is still no clarity on when or where a proposed memorandum of understanding between Washington and Tehran could be signed. The fragile ceasefire came under strain after Iran warned of retaliation following US "self-defense" strikes earlier this week. Inside Iran, authorities imposed fresh restrictions on messaging platforms and app stores after partially easing a nearly 90-day internet blackout, with many Iranians reacting with skepticism to the limited restoration of access. Meanwhile, tensions continued to rise in Lebanon as Israel issued new evacuation warnings for residents in southern Lebanon and intensified attacks on what it called Hezbollah infrastructure linked to Iran. Lebanese media reported multiple Israeli air raids across the Bekaa Valley near Baalbek and in several southern towns, including areas near Sidon and Jezzine. At least 31 people were killed in Israeli strikes on Tuesday, marking one of the deadliest days since the ceasefire took effect in April. Iran's Intelligence Ministry claimed its enemies had been defeated and were now relying on economic pressure, cyberattacks, assassinations and media campaigns to target the country. Follow IndiaToday.in for live updates.

sports.headline: Don't fast-track Sooryavanshi's India selection: Ex-batter issues warning Aakash Chopra has urged selectors not to fast-track Vaibhav Sooryavanshi into the India side after his latest IPL blitz. Sooryavanshi was the star of the show as RR defeated SRH in Mullanpur.

Chris Gayle crowns Vaibhav 'new six machine' after breaking iconic IPL record Universe Boss Chris Gayle crowned Vaibhav Sooryavanshi cricket's "new six machine" after the Rajasthan Royals teenager shattered his iconic IPL record for most sixes in a single season with a breathtaking display against Sunrisers Hyderabad in the Eliminator.

Coach quits via text message, leaves Spanish tennis star stunned at French Open Spain's Alejandro Davidovich Fokina was left stunned at the French Open after coach Mariano Puerta suddenly quit through a text message and flew to Miami without informing anyone on the team.

Baptiste suffers horrific knee injury at French Open, forced off on wheelchair Hailey Baptiste retired from her French Open second-round match after collapsing and injuring her left knee against Wang Xinyu. The distressing exit halted the best run of her career and raised fears of a long lay-off.

Fastest hundred missed, Vaibhav Sooryavanshi manifests IPL glory for RR Vaibhav Sooryavanshi missed out on the fastest hundred in IPL history by the narrowest of margins, but the Rajasthan Royals teenager made it clear that personal milestones are secondary as he remains focused on helping the franchise win its second IPL title.

Archer takes dig at PBKS after RR reach Qualifier 2: Don't want Punjab's fate Jofra Archer took a dig at Punjab Kings, saying that Rajasthan Royals don't want to get ahead of themselves and suffer the same fate as Shreyas Iyer and Co. PBKS crashed out of the play-offs race after a poor run of form in the second half of the campaign.

Kavya Maran in tears as SRH crash in IPL Eliminator vs Rajasthan Royals SunRisers Hyderabad owner Kavya Maran was nearly reduced to tears as her side crashed in the Eliminator of the Indian Premier League 2026.

Sooryavanshi 97 sends SunRisers home, Rajasthan closer to final Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's breathtaking 97 off 29 balls, laced with 12 sixes, set up Rajasthan Royals' 47-run win over SunRisers Hyderabad in the Eliminator in Mullanpur on Wednesday. RR will face Gujarat Titans on Friday in Qualifier 2.

Novak Djokovic matches Roger Federer's Open Era Grand Slam record at Roland Garros French Open 2026: Novak Djokovic beat Valentin Royer in four sets after the Frenchman stole the third-set tie-break on Chatrier. The win kept his pursuit of a 25th Grand Slam alive and exposed the resistance home support can still create.

Virat Kohli meets 2008 U19 World Cup teammate, daughter before IPL 2026 final Virat Kohli stepped off the team bus to meet former Under-19 teammate Taruwar Kohli and his daughter before the IPL 2026 final. The reunion, shared by Taruwar online, underlined a bond that survived sharply different careers and Kohli's rise to superstardom.

IOC mourns Randhir Singh's death, Olympic flag to fly at half-mast for three days The International Olympic Committee (IOC) on Wednesday mourned the death of veteran Indian sports administrator Randhir Singh, with the Olympic flag being flown at half-mast for three days at its headquarters here as a mark of respect.

Yuliia Starodubtseva delivers French Open shock, sends 2nd seed Elena Rybakina packingFrench Open 2026: Yuliia Starodubtseva beat second seed Elena Rybakina in a tense French Open second-round thriller. The upset blows open the women's draw and marks the biggest win of the Ukrainian's career.

Not just batting, Sooryavanshi's sanskar stands out in IPL Eliminator vs SRHSRH vs RR, IPL 2026: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's incredible batting was not the only thing that caught the eye on Wednesday. Ahead of the match vs SRH, Vaibhav sparked some hilarious reactions as he went to touch the feet of Sunil Gavaskar.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi foils SRH's Plan A, B, and C in IPL Eliminator15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi got off to a flyer against SunRisers Hyderabad in the IPL Eliminator on Wednesday. Vaibhav reached his fifty in just 16 balls, foiling all plans set by SRH in today's game.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi stands frozen on pitch after missing fastest IPL centuryIPL 2026 Eliminator, SRH vs RR: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi missed the record for the fastest century in the tournament when he was dismissed for 97 off 29 balls. The 15-year-old stood frozen in the middle, unable to believe his luck after he threw it away in the eighth over of the match in Mullanpur on Wednesday.

Vaibhav eclipses Chris Gayle, sets new IPL record for most sixes in a seasonVaibhav Sooryavanshi rewrote the IPL record books on Wednesday after breaking Chris Gayle's record for the most sixes in a single season during Rajasthan Royals' Eliminator clash against Sunrisers Hyderabad.

I had control over the game: Iga Swiatek cruises into French Open third roundIga Swiatek beat Sara Bejlek 6-2, 6-3 to reach the Roland-Garros third round. The score was tidy, but 38 unforced errors and a shaky serve showed there is still work to do in Paris.

The shooter who put India on the global trap map, inspired Olympians like Narang, BindraLong before India became a shooting powerhouse, Randhir Singh helped put the sport on the national map. He inspired generations of Olympians and transformed a little-known discipline into one of India's strongest Olympic pursuits.

Watch: Wasim Akram brings out iconic bowling action during Hajj RamiA viral video from Mecca showed Wasim Akram performing the Rami al-Jamarat ritual with the same compact, wristy release cricket fans know by heart. The clip drew laughter around him and nostalgia online, reminding viewers how completely his bowling action still belongs to the game's memory.

French Open: Tamara Korpatsch vs Wang Xinyu ends with no handshake dramaTamara Korpatsch beat Wang Xinyu in a tense three-set French Open second-round match. A line-call dispute and the refusal to shake hands turned the result into a wider talking point.

Paralympic champion Sumit Antil sets new javelin world recordSumit Antil broke his own F64 javelin world record with a 74.82m throw in Bengaluru. The mark underlined his sustained dominance and set the tone for his Asian Para Games push.

Hardik Pandya deletes Instagram after disastrous MI season, restores it hours laterIndia all-rounder and Mumbai Indians captain Hardik Pandya's Instagram account briefly disappeared before returning online hours later. The account was restored within a few hours, although Pandya did not issue any clarification regarding the sudden disappearance.

Manchester United back to generating profit after securing UCL footballManchester United posted a 37.7 million pound operating profit for the nine months ending March 31, 2026. Driven by cuts, compliance and a top-three finish, means that United are on track to complete a major financial turnaround.

Dhoni's CSK future rests with him, says team CEOIPL 2026: CSK CEO Kasi Viswanathan said MS Dhoni alone will decide his future with the franchise. The remarks keep open roles as player, coach or mentor as Chennai continue their transition.

SRH vs RR Highlights: Rajasthan thrash Hyderabad to reach Qualifier 2Hyderabad (SRH) vs Rajasthan (RR) Highlights, IPL 2026 Eliminator: Rajasthan make it to Qualifier 2 as they thrash Hyderabad by 47 runs. The win was set up by Sooryavanshi's 97 and then Jofra Archer's three-wicket burst in the powerplay.

Norway Chess: Gukesh, Praggnanandhaa lose as Divya continues her winning runGukesh and Praggnanandhaa let promising Norway Chess positions drift into Armageddon defeats. Divya Deshmukh kept India in the conversation, while Firouzja and Carlsen underlined how brutally this format punishes hesitation.

Singapore Open: HS Prannoy stages brilliant fightback to dump out Jonatan ChristieSingapore Open: HS Prannoy came from a game down to beat fifth seed Jonatan Christie at the Singapore Open. The comeback sent him into the Round of 16, where he will face Kidambi Srikanth or Loh Kean Yew.

Randhir Singh, India's first Asian Games shooting gold medallist, dies at 79Indian sport mourns Randhir Singh, India's first Asian Games shooting gold medallist and veteran sports administrator, whose legacy spanned five Olympics and the global Olympic movement.

French Open, Day 4 LIVE: Djokovic advances to third round as Rybakina crashes outFrench Open 2026, Day 4 Live updates: Novak Djokovic sealed his spot in the third round of the French Open with 6-3, 6-2, 6-7, 6-3 win over Valentin Royer. Iga Swiatek cruised past Sara Bejlek 6-2, 6-3 to continue her title charge, while reigning Australian Open champion Elena Rybakina suffered a shock second-round exit after losing to Yuliia Starodubtseva.

SRH have Plan A, B and C ready for Vaibhav Sooryavanshi: Pat CumminsIPL 2026: Sunrisers Hyderabad captain Pat Cummins admitted that SRH have prepared multiple bowling plans for Rajasthan Royals teenage sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi ahead of the Eliminator clash. The SRH skipper reflected on Vaibhav's stunning century against SRH earlier this season and said Hyderabad's bowlers have learned important lessons from that defeat.

Sinner's French Open pursuit hits a snag. It's got nothing to do with tennisRoland Garros: World Number One Jannik Sinner began his French Open campaign with a dominant straight-sets victory over Clément Tabur, extending his incredible winning streak to 30 consecutive matches. But his biggest challenge is not even on the clay court.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
